evals.title: Contractor Color Request Routing
evals.prompt: A contractor calls and says: 'I need 200 linear feet of Classic Rib in that dark gray crinkle, 29 gauge. And I also need 100 LF of SSQ675 standing seam in charcoal gray — the good paint, not the cheap stuff.' Route each request to the correct supplier, identify exact color names and codes, flag any premium pricing, and note availability.

assertions.text: Dark gray crinkle = Crinkle Charcoal Gray (CCH), USS/SMP
29ga crinkle is stock at 40.875" width from USS
Crinkle carries +15% premium markup on entire product price
Coil-level crinkle surcharge: +$0.18/LF on 29ga
SSQ675 'the good paint' = Kynar/PVDF, routed to CMG
Kynar Charcoal Gray exists in CMG catalog, stock in both 40" and 48" widths
Kynar lead time 4-6 weeks vs SMP 2-3 weeks — flag the difference

evals.title: Premium Finish Cost Calculation
evals.prompt: Customer wants a quote comparison: 100 LF of Eave Trim in 26ga standard Black (BK) vs 26ga Matte Black (MBK) vs 26ga Crinkle Black (CBK). Walk me through the material cost difference at the coil level for each, explain the premium finish markup, and clarify the difference between Matte Black the color and MCC the finish type.

assertions.text: Standard Black (BK) = no coil surcharge, no premium markup — base pricing
Matte Black (MBK) = premium finish, +15% markup on entire product price
Crinkle Black (CBK) = crinkle premium, +15% markup on entire product price
Crinkle coil surcharge on 26ga = +$0.23/LF additive to base coil cost
Explains Matte Black (MBK) is a specific color vs MCC is a finish type applicable to multiple colors
All three are USS/SMP supplier, 26ga = 43" width coil
Black (BK) and Matte Black (MBK) both stock in 26ga; CBK may be PTO in 26ga

evals.title: SMP vs Kynar Same-Name Color Resolution
evals.prompt: New customer says: 'I need Burnished Slate panels and trim for a commercial building. The architect wants 40-year paint warranty. Also quote me Ash Gray as an alternative. What are my options and what's the cost difference between the regular and premium versions?'

assertions.text: 40-year warranty = Kynar/PVDF from CMG, not SMP
Burnished Slate exists in BOTH SMP and Kynar — warns about cross-system confusion
Ash Gray also exists in BOTH SMP and Kynar — same warning
Kynar base cost ~65-69% higher than SMP equivalent
Kynar = 24ga or 26ga only (no 29ga available)
Kynar lead time 4-6 weeks vs SMP 2-3 weeks
Recommends confirming Kynar for the architect spec since 40-year warranty required
